title: After shutdown, federal employees face new uncertainty: affording health insurance
summary: A sign, seen July 11, 2025, directs visitors to the entrance of Alaska Regional Hospital entrance and adjacent medical buildings. (Photo by Yereth Rosen/Alaska Beacon) Larry Humphreys, a retired Federal Emergency Management Agency worker in Moultrie, Georgia, says he and his wife won’t be traveling much next year after their monthly health insurance premium payment increases more than 40%, to $938. Larry Humphreys, a retired federal worker, has insurance for himself and his wife, Julianne, through the Federal Employees Health Benefits Program. They have several health plan options through the program but have stayed with the same one for decades. (Photo courtesy of Larry Humphreys) Humphreys, 68, feels betrayed by the Federal Employees Health Benefits Program. “As federal employees, we sacrificed good salaries in the private sector because we thought the benefits from governmen...

title: Antofagasta’s transport division launches Latin America’s first hydrogen locomotive
summary: FCAB (Ferrocarril de Antofagasta a Bolivia), Antofagasta Plc’s transport division, marked its 137th anniversary with an operational milestone: becoming the first freight rail operator in Latin America to launch a hydrogen-powered locomotive. Powered by hydrogen produced from renewable sources, the l...

title: Cordova finishes third at conference volleyball playoffs
summary: Cordova finished third at the conference volleyball playoffs in Anchorage on Nov. 20-22. Unalaska took first place for the second year in a row, and SuValley came in second, also for the second year in a row. Both will advance to the state finals. Cordova improved from fourth place last year to thir...

title: AST Attempted Murder, Misconduct Involving Weapons, Assault: Wasilla [AK25118793]
incident_type: Attempted Murder, Misconduct Involving Weapons, Assault
summary: On 11/27/25 at approximately 1718 hours, the Alaska State Troopers in Palmer received a report of a road rage incident on Knik Goose Bay Road near Carmel Road. The complainant stated a vehicle passed him aggressively and then pulled to the side of the road. As the complainant passed, the driver of the vehicle opened the driver door and pointed a handgun at the complainant. As the complainant drove past, the other driver fired two rounds at the complainant, striking his vehicle once. Through investigation, troopers identified 66-year-old Ronald Hand as the suspect. He was contacted at his residence and arrested for Attempted Murder in the Second Degree, Misconduct Involving Weapons in the First Degree, Misconduct Involving Weapons in the Third Degree, Assault in the Third Degree, and Crimin
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Attempted Murder, Misconduct Involving Weapons, Assault

entities.address_detail: Knik Goose Bay Road near Carmel Road

title: OPINION: Why does Mat-Su have the lowest law enforcement coverage in Alaska?
summary: The Matanuska Susitna Borough is no longer some sleepy frontier outpost. It is Alaska’s fastest growing region, now home to 118,773 people, more than the entire Fairbanks North Star Borough and rapidly closing in on Anchorage. Roughly 101,873 of those residents live outside Wasilla and Palmer. They...

title: Site visit: Minaurum eyes first resource at Alamos in Mexico within weeks
summary: On a plateau high up in the Alamos mountain ranges of Mexico’s Sonora state, Stephen Maynard casts a sweeping gesture across the valley, indicating where the two main shoots of the Promontorio vein zone intersect at the Alamos silver project. Maynard, the vice-president of exploration at Minaurum Gold (TSXV: MGG; US-OTC: MMRGF), sees the potential as the company targets an initial resource by year’s end that might hold 50 million oz. silver-equivalent oz. grading over 300 grams per ton. Four rigs were turning during a November site visit. “The dual zone runs right down through the valley – the vein zones are all in the footwall side of that structure,” the executive said, noting Minauru...

title: Allied discovers five new targets at Ethiopia gold project
summary: Allied Gold (TSX, NYSE: AAUC) said recent drilling at its Kurmuk property in western Ethiopia has identified at least five new target areas, underpinning plans for a high-volume gold operation. The stock hit an all-time high. Hole TSDD041 in the Tsenge–Hiccup Hill zone cut 16.4 metres grading 13 gra...

summary: The $28 million first phase would extend Glacier Highway and prepare the site on Goldbelt land.

title: Pro-lifers to sing Christmas carols outside Fairbanks abortion clinic
summary: As the world prepares to celebrate the birth of Jesus Christ, Fairbanks area pro-lifers are planning to affirm the inherent dignity of unborn babies by singing Christmas songs outside the local Planned Parenthood facility. Set for Dec. 10, there will be two caroling events, 1-2 p.m. and 4-5 p.m. The...

title: Homeland Security wants state driver’s license data for sweeping citizenship program
summary: A California Highway Patrol officer talks to a driver during a traffic stop in October. The U.S. Department of Homeland Security wants access to state driver’s license data as it builds a powerful citizenship verification program. (Photo by Justin Sullivan/Getty Images) The Trump administration wants access to state driver’s license data on millions of U.S. residents as it builds a powerful citizenship verification program amid its clampdown on voter fraud and illegal immigration. The U.S. Department of Homeland Security seeks access to an obscure computer network used by law enforcement agencies, according to a federal notice, potentially allowing officials to bypass negotiating with states for the records. The information would then be plugged into a Homeland Security program known as SAVE that Trump officials have deployed to search for rare instances of alleged noncitizen voters and to verify citizenship. The plan comes as the Trump administration demands states share copies of the...

title: National Guard shooting suspect to face murder charge
summary: A small memorial of flowers and an American flag outside the Farragut West Metro station in Washington, D.C., near where two members of the West Virginia National Guard were shot on Nov. 26. (Photo by Andrew Leyden/Getty Images) The United States Attorney’s Office for the District of Columbia announced Friday it has charged the man who allegedly shot two National Guard members earlier this week with first-degree murder after one of the soldiers died as a result of her injuries. summary: Take Five is The Arctic Institute's weekly redux on the Arctic's most important stories, published every Friday. The post The Arctic This Week Take Five: Week of 24 November, 2025 appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .

title: Give the gift of healthy skin, from glaciers
summary: by Tim Lydon It’s been over twenty years, but Lauren Padawer still remembers the moment she fell in love with Copper River mud. While visiting Alaska for a rafting trip, her group stopped for lunch and a midday dip in the river’s cold-water eddies. She was mesmerized when her bare feet sank into the...

title: Trump administration proposes offshore leasing in almost all Alaska waters
summary: This article was originally published in the Alaska Beacon. Yereth Rosen Alaska Beacon The Trump administration on Nov. 20 released a plan for offshore oil and gas leasing that would open up almost all Alaska marine waters to development, along with the entire Pacific coast and the Gulf of Mexico. The Alaska portion of the plan proposes 21 lease sales through 2031, five of them in Cook Inlet, two in the Beaufort Sea, two in the Chukchi Sea and the others in other marine areas. Those include a lease sale in a newly designated “ High Arctic ” area that lies beyond the 200-nautical-mile exclusive economic zone and where U.S. territorial rights are not yet clear. The only federal Alaska offshore area without a proposed lease sale is the North Aleutian Basin, where oil leasing is under an indefinite ban to protect salmon-rich Bristol Bay. The new plan, which also proposes six oil lease sales off the U.S. West Coast and seven in the Gulf of Mexico, is similar to an offshore leasing plan prop...
